stageHeight.name: Stage Height (%)
stageHeight.hint: Height of the visual novel stage area as a percentage of the viewport height.

stageWidth.name: Stage Width (%)
stageWidth.hint: Width of the visual novel stage area as a percentage of the viewport width.

commsTheme.name: Comms Theme
commsTheme.hint: Visual style for the call-in overlay.

commsEdge.name: Comms Edge
commsEdge.hint: Which screen edge the call-in cards stack against.

commsVAlign.name: Comms Vertical Anchor
commsVAlign.hint: Where the call-in stack anchors vertically. The vertical offset below is measured relative to this anchor.

commsFrameWidth.name: Comms Card Width (px)
commsFrameWidth.hint: Width of each call-in portrait card in pixels.

commsEdgeOffset.name: Comms Edge Spacing (px)
commsEdgeOffset.hint: Gap between the call-in stack and its screen edge, in pixels.

commsTopOffset.name: Comms Vertical Offset (%)
commsTopOffset.hint: Vertical offset relative to the anchor, as a percentage of viewport height. When centered it nudges up (negative) or down (positive); when anchored from the top or bottom it sets the gap from that edge.

panel.title: Stage Director
panel.noSelectedToken: Select one or more tokens on the canvas first.
panel.noFoundryActorSelected: Select a Foundry Actor to import first.
panel.name: Name
panel.measureInfo: Preview all actors side by side to calibrate scale and positioning. Adjust each actor so feet are level on the floor line.
panel.stageYOffsetHint: Percentage of character to crop from the bottom. The visible portion scales up to fill the stage.
panel.noActorsConfigured: No actors configured. Click Add Actor or import one to get started.
panel.noSlotsConfigured: No slots on stage. Click Add Slot to create character positions.
panel.noActorsToMeasure: No actors configured. Add actors in the Actors tab first.
panel.clearCommsConfirm: End all active calls and hide the comms overlay?
panel.noCalls: No active calls. Ring an actor in from the Actors tab or the dropdown above.
panel.commsFraming: Position the portrait inside the call card (scale / X / Y)
panel.commsThemeOverride: Override the comms theme for this actor
panel.commsTintOverride: Enable a custom tint colour for this actor

guide.title: Stage Setup Guide
guide.intro: Configure your actors to display consistently across all player screens.
guide.step1: Click 'Add Actor' to create characters. Give them a name and select their image. Use character art with transparent backgrounds for best results.
guide.step2: Adjust Scale to resize the actor (1.0 = 100%). Use Offset X/Y to fine-tune positioning within the stage. These values sync to all players.
guide.step3: Switch to the Stage tab to control what players see. Add slots (positions) for characters. Assign actors to slots or leave them empty for spacing.
guide.step4: Click Highlight on a character to dim others and indicate the speaker. Use animations for expressive moments — bounce for excitement, shake for anger, nod for agreement.
guide.tip: Tip: Use 'Show Stage' to make the stage visible to all players. 'Clear Stage' removes all characters but keeps actors configured.
